stories.title: EU fails to strike Russia sanctions deal after 3 days of talks
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries failed for a third consecutive day on Wednesday to agree on a new package of sanctions against Russia, forcing them to temporarily freeze the bloc’s price cap on Russian crude for another week. Ambassadors deferred further discussions to July 22 while agreeing to temporarily maintain the oil price cap at $44.10 per barrel until July 23, two EU diplomats familiar with the matter said. Ireland, which currently holds the rotating presidency of the Council of the EU, sch

stories.title: Moscow warns that foreign troops in Ukraine would be seen as legitimate targets – as it happened
stories.summary: Members of the pro-Ukraine “coalition of the willing” this week reasserted their desire for such a force after a cessation of hostilities Meanwhile, the Kremlin has rejected Lithuanian president’s warnings about a potential Russian attack on critical infrastructure ( 10:06 ) , dismissing them as “horror stories” intended to “prepare public for further militarisation.” Reuters reported that the Kremlin said these comments were merely a “pretext for further deployment of Nato military infrastructu

stories.title: Most realistic ancient roman faces revealed at Budapest exhibition
stories.summary: Sixteen faces from Roman Aquincum have been brought back to life in a new exhibition, where DNA analysis and forensic anthropology reveal the diverse backgrounds of the ancient city's inhabitants.

stories.title: Russian attacks kill eight as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
stories.summary: Civilian casualties are reported in four Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

stories.title: Top Orbán ally joins Chinese car giant BYD
stories.summary: Former Hungarian Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó announced Wednesday he had resigned from parliament to take a senior executive role at Chinese electric vehicle giant BYD. Szijjártó said on Facebook he had accepted “a highly prestigious offer” from the company, and will become “the executive responsible for the group’s external relations and the development of new business lines.” Prime Minister Péter Magyar — who ousted his predecessor Viktor Orbán in the country’s Apri

stories.title: Watch: The clash between US and Iran for control of the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The BBC's Barbara Plett Usher examines the latest tensions over the key international shipping channel.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergely az RMDSZ elnökével, Kelemen Hunorral beszélget Tusványoson
stories.summary: Idén is hosszú a fideszes meghívottak listája Tusványoson, Orbán Viktor mellett Gulyás Gergellyel, Deutsch Tamással és Németh Zsolttal is lehet majd találkozni, ahogy Bohár Dániel és Bayer Zsolt is ott lesz.

stories.title: Pócs János: Magyar Péter nagyobb demokráciát ígért, de a Fidesz-kormány alatt sosem voltak kitiltva a mobilok
stories.summary: A fideszes képviselő szerint ők nem kényszerítették ilyen „óvodás szintre” a politikusokat.

stories.summary: A volt külügyminiszter a BYD külső kapcsolataiért és az új üzletágak fejlesztéséért felelős vezetőjeként dolgozik tovább.

stories.title: Former Hungarian FM Szijjártó resigns seat in Parliament
stories.summary: The former foreign minister will be working as BYD's head of external relations and developing new business divisions.

stories.title: Szijjártó Péter kétszer is egyeztetett Orbánnal a lemondásáról
stories.summary: A párt sajtófőnöke barátsággal gondol vissza a közös munkára a volt külügyminiszterrel.

stories.title: Pósfai Gábor kabinetfőnöke lett Szabó Bence
stories.summary: Méghozzá ezredesként, három hét alatt három rendfokozatot ugrott.

stories.title: Szijjártó Péter nem hagyja el az országot, de többet fog utazni a BYD-s pozíciójában, mint külügyminiszterként
stories.summary: Információink szerint a parlamenti mandátumáról lemondó volt politikust több nagyvállalat is megkereste, és hetekkel a lemondása előtt szólt a döntésről Orbán Viktornak.

stories.title: Magyar Péter reagált Szijjártó Péter lemondására
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint folytatódik a Fidesz szétesése.

stories.title: Navracsics: A magyar közvélemény nagyobbik része úgy közelít a Fideszhez, mint bűnözők társaságához
stories.summary: Az ötödik Orbán-kormány területfejlesztési minisztere azt mondta, Orbánnak szerinte pihennie kell, és kerülte a választ arra a kérdésre, hogy van-e szándék új párt létrehozására.

stories.title: This is not just about the crisis of Fidesz; it also marks the end of many politicians' careers
stories.summary: Many in Fidesz have now realized that the “Magyar era” has begun, and they will not be the ones calling shots for more than just the next four years. Some MPs are waiting for Tisza's approval ratings to decline, while others are calling that naive. We spoke with Fidesz politicians in the wake of Gergely Gulyás’s resignation.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor a nyár csúcsigazolásának nevezte, hogy Szijjártó a BYD-nél fog dolgozni
stories.summary: „Hajrá Péter!” – írta Facebook-oldalán a volt miniszterelnök.

stories.summary: A rendőrök 353 gramm kristályt találtak egy autóban, amiben a képviselő utazott. A drogtesztje is pozitív lett.

stories.summary: „Mi maradunk a honfitársainkkal” – írta posztjában a Mi Hazánk frakcióvezetője.

stories.summary: A kórház közleménye szerint nem higiéniai hiányosságok vezettek a helyzethez.

stories.title: „Gulyás érezte a felelősségét, hogy egy ilyen embert szabadított a magyar közéletre”
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor szabad ember, és nem is biztos, hogy ő lesz az ellenzék vezetője. Gulyás Gergely érzékenyebb lelkű, ezért is mondhatott le a frakcióvezetőségről. Ilyeneket tudhattunk meg a héten a parlamentben.

stories.title: Gibraltár a schengeni övezet része lett, lebontották a határkerítést
stories.summary: A határellenőrzés felelőssége Spanyolországhoz került.

stories.title: Bőkezűen támogatta Szijjártó minisztériuma a BYD-t, a cég pedig most meg is hálálja neki
stories.summary: A volt külügyminiszter aktív támogatásával a BYD öt új üzemet és központot nyitott Magyarországon kevesebb mint 10 év alatt, ehhez pedig több tízmilliárd forint támogatást nyújtott a kormány közpénzből. Majd Szijjártó a Fidesz bukása után állást kapott a cégtől.

stories.summary: Közös levelük szerint az utcákon egyre több a probléma, amit a kormánynak kell kezelnie.

stories.title: Orbán János Dénes ügyének tisztázását követeli az Operettszínház társulata
stories.summary: A színház vezetése korábban forráshiányra panaszkodott, eközben a szakmai vezető Orbán nagy összegű közpénzt kapott jogdíjként.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a korrupció legvérlázítóbb fajtájának tartja, hogy Szijjártó a BYD-hoz szerződött
stories.summary: Szerinte morális és súlyos jogi aggályokat is felvet, hogy annál a cégnél lesz vezető a volt külügyminiszter, amit korábban százmilliárdokkal támogatott.

stories.title: Vasvillával rongált autókat, majd ásóval támadt rendőrökre egy férfi Mikepércsen
stories.summary: A rendőröknek sokkolót kellett használniuk az agresszív férfi ellen.

stories.summary: Döntését azzal indokolta, hogy az őt támadó kampány a teljes magyar atlétikai közösségnek okozott kárt.

stories.title: Poland could cut arms aid to Ukraine as WWII spat continues
stories.summary: As Poland observes a day of remembrance for the victims of a Ukrainian nationalist military unit, there are increasing signs that it could cut off its neighbor from military aid, even as the war against Russia continues.

stories.title: Merz to MAGA: Butt out of Europe’s elections
stories.summary: BERLIN — German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Wednesday pushed back against a new, million-dollar funding scheme from U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration to support “free speech and religious liberty” in Europe. The funding comes against the backdrop of the U.S.’s efforts to support nationalist parties in Europe as part of its National Security Strategy and burgeoning links between the Trump administration and Europe’s far right, including the Alternative for Germany (A

stories.title: Arrest of American who has given $1m in aid to Palestine prompts fears of ‘growing repression from Trump’
stories.summary: Extradition request for James ‘Fergie’ Chambers sparks concerns that US government is conflating humanitarian aid with terrorism The arrest in Ibiza and US justice department extradition request for a wealthy American donor to leftwing causes in connection with alleged material support for Hamas is sending ripples through leftwing circles in Spain and being closely monitored in the US for potential “chilling effects” on support for Palestine. Spanish authorities detained James “Fergie” Chambers

stories.summary: Prime minister’s plan will create energy regulations for datacentres in Australia Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Environmental and community groups have called for a pause on datacentre development until new regulations are in place after Anthony Albanese promised “greater clarity and speed” over their approval. stories.title: Todd Blanche downplays Trump alliance in confirmation hearing
stories.summary: President’s pick for attorney general faces tough questions over purging of career prosecutors, January 6 and Epstein Todd Blanche sought to downplay his close relationship with Donald Trump , tried to distance himself from decisions regarding January 6 rioters, and defended his handling of files regarding Jeffrey Epstein as well a settlement agreement that created a $1.8bn slush fund and giving the president and his family immunity from audits during his confirmation hearing to be the next atto

stories.title: Trump rails against New York’s statewide datacenter moratorium
stories.summary: AI-friendly president shared a post saying governor Kathy Hochul should scrap the one-year policy ‘IMMEDIATELY’ Donald Trump railed against the New York governor, Kathy Hochul, for pausing the construction of large new datacenters, the resource-intensive facilities that power artificial intelligence. New York became the first US state to enact a moratorium on new datacenters on Tuesday, when Hochul signed an executive order mandating a one-year statewide pause on so-called “hyperscale” datacente

stories.title: US resumes daylight strikes on Iran as tensions escalate over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US Central Command said it has resumed daylight strikes on Iran, targeting coastal defences and missile sites. Tehran has reported casualties as tensions rise over a renewed blockade threat, calls for retaliation and the ongoing dispute over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran threatens to block more trade routes as US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran's bridges and power plants next week if the country does not return to talks.

stories.title: Musk says Le Pen is ‘France’s last hope’ ahead of 2027 election
stories.summary: PARIS — The world’s richest man has given the leader of the French far right his quasi-endorsement ahead of the country’s crucial presidential election next year. “She is France’s last hope,” wrote Elon Musk, the owner of X, on the social media platform Wednesday, in a post referring to far-right presidential candidate Marine Le Pen’s favorable polling figures. Le Pen formally launched her fourth bid for president last week after being cleared to run by an appeals c

stories.title: Keir Starmer leaves Andy Burnham to figure out kids’ social media ban
stories.summary: LONDON — One of Keir Starmer’s final acts before he announced his resignation as prime minister was to unveil a social media ban for under-16s . As Starmer prepares to hand over power to prime-minister-in-waiting Andy Burnham, he’ll also be handing over the hardest part of the ban — figuring out how it will actually work.  Starmer’s Tech Secretary Liz Kendall gave an update on Wednesday, filling in some of the gaps on the policy since Starmer’s t

stories.title: Man arrested over social media threat to Nigel Farage
stories.summary: LONDON — A man was arrested Tuesday after an alleged threat to Nigel Farage on social media that he would be shot in the head. In a statement, the Met Police said they arrested a man in his 20s “on suspicion of sending threatening communications to a Member of Parliament.” That man was released on bail Wednesday. A report in the Telegraph newspaper alleges the Reform UK leader was told: “I am going to shoot you in the head if you win.” Police said the arrest relates to a social media

stories.title: French parliament legalizes assisted dying
stories.summary: PARIS — French lawmakers voted on Wednesday to legalize assisted dying for terminally ill patients, bringing to an end a fraught, months-long process that sowed deep divisions within every political camp. Patients over the age of 18 suffering from a “serious and incurable” illness that is life-threatening and has reached an advanced, irreversible or terminal stage will be allowed to request assistance in dying, but only if their illness causes “unbearable” suffering and t

stories.title: Iran launches strikes on neighbours as Trump threatens to hit power plants
stories.summary: Tehran's attack comes as the US military carried out a fourth night of strikes against Iran after President Donald Trump reinstated its blockade of Iranian shipping around the strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: China’s economy grows at 4.3%, one of its lowest rates on record
stories.summary: Worse-than-expected figures for three months to June come amid concerns over lopsided economy Business live – latest updates China has posted worse-than-expected growth figures for the three months to June as its economy expanded by just 4.3% – one of its lowest quarterly readings on record. The rate, which came in under the government’s target of 4.5% to 5%, was one of the weakest since reporting on official quarterly GDP figures began in the early 1990s. Continue reading...

stories.title: Almost 145,000 Australians will lose support for autism under NDIS reforms, documents reveal
stories.summary: Internal estimates show 144,600 people will be taken off the scheme by 2030, many of them under 18 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Almost 145,000 Australians receiving support for autism on the national disability insurance scheme (NDIS) are expected to be removed by the end of the decade, internal estimates show, as the federal government moves ahead with plans to narrow the focus of the “diluted” $52bn scheme toward those with significant and complex needs. stories.summary: Challenges of regulating social media or stopping hate speech show these firms can set their own terms and prices for countries like Australia Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese took a trip back in time during his much anticipated speech on artificial intelligence on Wednesday. stories.title: Save the Children clashes with Labour after accusing Starmer of ‘complicity’ in Gaza deaths
stories.summary: Government – which provides significant portion of charity’s funding – is understood to have demanded an explanation The charity Save the Children has angered the government with a social media post marking Keir Starmer’s impending exit from Downing Street. The organisation suggested on X that the outgoing prime minister was complicit in the deaths of thousands of civilians in the Israel-Gaza war. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany warns against election interference as US offers funding to Maga-aligned causes in Europe
stories.summary: New state department scheme will provide grants to ‘address sovereignty, migration, censorship and lawfare’ Friedrich Merz has warned Donald Trump’s administration against interfering in German elections after the US state department announced a scheme to fund Maga-aligned causes in Europe. The German chancellor was responding to a new US initiative offering grants of up to $3m (£2.2m) for European charities, thinktanks and individuals. Continue reading...

stories.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay ministers $356,000 in defamation case
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s editor-in-chief, John Micklethwait, stands by reporting, saying ministers who sued ‘imposed an extremely strained meaning on what was a solid ​story’ Bloomberg News and one of its reporters have been ordered to pay S$460,000 (US$355,734) in damages after an article it published was found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers, the city-state’s high court said in a judgment released on Tuesday. Bloomberg and the reporter, Low De Wei, are liable to jointly pay S$230,000 to

stories.title: US Fifth Fleet targeted in Bahrain, says Iran, in latest exchange of strikes – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is closed. You can read our latest full report here Resurgent oil and fuel prices could cement a fourth interest rate rise in Australia this year if Donald Trump’s renewed conflict with Iran is not resolved within a week , economists warn. US missile strikes on Iran and Trump’s announcement of a new maritime blockade has lifted oil prices to their highest point in the month since the two countries agreed to a peace deal . Continue reading...

stories.title: India news: Landmark trade deal with UK takes effect
stories.summary: The free trade agreement allows zero-duty market access for nearly 99% of India's exports to the UK. Meanwhile, US senators have proposed a new bill to impose 100% tariffs on India for buying oil from Russia.

stories.title: Ukraine: Kyiv and EU agree on new drone production deal
stories.summary: Ukraine and the EU have agreed on a drone deal combining Kyiv’s know-how with the bloc's scale-up power. Meanwhile, Russian attacks caused more fatalities in Odesa as Moscow increasingly targets the port. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition has 'found its footing,' Merz says
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said recent reforms show his coalition is making progress. Meanwhile, arrests were made after police carry out drug raids in western Germany and the Netherlands. DW has the latest.

stories.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
stories.summary: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

stories.title: Iran: Alleged Ahmadinejad plot highlights info war
stories.summary: Reports that Israel explored a scenario to effect regime change involving former Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad reveal how the Iran war has also become a struggle over narratives and perception.

stories.title: Boss of fake government agency arrested in Nigeria after weeks on the run
stories.summary: The case has gripped Nigeria since President Bola Tinubu ordered a corruption investigation last week.

stories.summary: Ernest Bai Koroma was arrested in 2023 after gunmen broke into an armoury and some jails, freeing inmates.

stories.title: ICE should continue traffic stops after shootings, Trump says, apparently contradicting new policy

stories.summary: The March and March provincial leader died of his injuries in hospital several days after being shot.

stories.title: Shipwrecks of Shackleton and Scott recreated in 3D digital form after deep sea expedition
stories.summary: Canadian scientists visit remains of polar exploration vessels in ‘golden era for shipwreck investigating’ Moments after devouring the final glimmers of light, the seafloor offered nothing but darkness and silt. Then the bow appeared. More than 1,000ft (305 metres) below the surface of the Labrador Sea, off the coast of Canada, the skeleton of the final ship used by the famed polar explorer Ernest Shackleton appeared in its silty grave. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Collective failure’: French press digests World Cup semi-final defeat by Spain
stories.summary: L’Équipe laments ‘mentally sunk’ players in Dallas Ouest France front page reads: ‘End of American dream’ French fans’ expectations of their national team had been at an all‑time high: a semi-final on Bastille Day, Kylian Mbappé a hero, a squad unbeaten. Across the country, bars had been packed with viewers spilling on to pavements, ready to crack open the fireworks ahead of hopes for the final. The night was unexpectedly subdued, however, streets cleared early. On Wednesday morning, the French

stories.title: Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks
stories.summary: Nobel laureate María Corina Machado will not lead negotiations over new elections, contrary to expectations The interim government of Venezuela has announced it will begin formal talks with the opposition aimed at “strengthening democracy” in the country. The move is backed by the US, which says it is seeking a “democratic transition” in a country still recovering from the twin earthquakes that killed more than 4,700 people. Continue reading...

stories.title: Surviving extreme heat increasingly boils down to this: access to air conditioning | Mark Wolfe
stories.summary: The next great climate divide will be between countries that have the resources to adapt and those that don’t This summer, much of the media’s attention has focused on record temperatures across Europe and the United States. Television coverage has been filled with familiar images: heat maps shaded deep red, schools closing, rail lines slowing, wildfires spreading and emergency rooms treating growing numbers of people with heat-related illnesses. stories.summary: Anna Liedtke files criminal complaint in Israel over alleged attack by female guards and says abuse was intended to silence campaigners The third time Anna Liedtke was subjected to an illegal strip-search in Israeli detention, female prison guards forced her on to her knees, covered her mouth to stop her screaming and raped her, according to interviews and a criminal complaint filed in Israel. stories.title: Meloni government vows to press on with electoral reform after losing key vote

stories.title: Berlin man charged with 22 counts of raping unconscious women and filming the assaults
stories.summary: Assailant allegedly sedated victims using sleeping tablets and alcohol after meeting them online A Berlin man has been charged with nearly two dozen counts of raping unconscious women and filming the acts, while investigators believe based on video evidence that the suspect may have attacked up to 60 victims. stories.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
stories.summary: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellow Smoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US. stories.title: Chaos and confusion bring US no closer to resolution on strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Five months of U-turns and false boasts leave Donald Trump in worse position than when he started Donald Trump has taken the war with Iran into a new, murkier phase as the two sides move further and further from the vague memorandum of understanding (MoU) signed on 17 June. And as during the opening phase of the conflict, the US leader’s objectives and methods are clouded in confusion, daily U-turns and boasts that within hours are revealed to be false. stories.title: Extreme temperature warnings in place as ‘heat dome’ bakes US
stories.summary: Warnings about dangerous, unhealthy air also extended from Minnesota to New York as wildfires rage in Toronto Extreme heat warnings are in place for large swaths of the United States this week, as an unusual weather pattern eclipses record temperature highs. In Billings, Montana, residents experienced a sweltering 111F (43.9C) on Sunday, the hottest day in its history. Salt Lake City in Utah also hit an all-time record high on Sunday at 109F. stories.title: Andy Burnham signals a wealth tax is off the agenda for now
stories.summary: Incoming prime minister says he doesn’t want to ‘create new divisions’ as Mahmood is tipped to be chancellor Andy Burnham has signalled that he will not increase wealth taxes immediately after becoming prime minister, easing concerns among the business community but limiting his economic room for manoeuvre. stories.title: Jude Bellingham lookalikes compete for a £1,966 Deliveroo voucher
stories.summary: The match-winning England striker is ‘a good ambassador for young people’, says one contestant All together now, with arms in the air: One Jude Bellingham, there’s only one Jude Bellingham … Unless, that is, you happened to find yourself in Shoreditch, east London, on Wednesday, where a dozen young men who looked vaguely like the England midfielder gathered for a lookalike contest almost as competitive as the one currently continuing in the US. stories.title: Palestinians in Gaza: Stuck between Hamas and Israel

stories.title: Why investors are suddenly fleeing Indonesia

stories.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
stories.summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

stories.title: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafari bid for religious freedom marijuana exemption
stories.summary: Kenya's small Rastafarian community was seeking legal permission to smoke marijuana under the constitutional right to freedom of religion. The judge found they did not show it to be a necessary part of their worship.

stories.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
stories.summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AFD party.

stories.summary: The US military announced a new wave of attacks on Iran, it says is aimed at "degrading" Iranian military capability. The strikes come after the US military said it would begin enforcing a naval blockade. DW has more.

stories.summary: Authorities did not know if the boat was headed to Alcatraz, a major tourist attraction, where shuttered buildings of the famous former prison still stand.

stories.title: Iran executes man over January protests as crackdown on dissent widens
stories.summary: Mohammad Amini Dehaghani was convicted of setting fire to a governor's office in Isfahan on 9 January, as Amnesty International says Iran is using wartime conditions as a pretext to accelerate politically motivated executions.

stories.title: Air India crash report likely to be ready in October, say investigators

stories.title: Celebrations as Gibraltar-Spain border fence removed

stories.title: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires

stories.title: Italy: melting ice statues protest heatwave conditions in Rome

stories.title: Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa

stories.title: Border controls scrapped between Spain and Gibraltar

A tavalyi sikertelen kilakoltatási kísérlet után idén már nem állt ellen a végrehajtónak, de több tucat állatát ott hagyta kritikus körülmények között, ezeket most állatorvosok gondozzák, a költségeket pedig a fővárosnak kell viselnie.

stories.title: France holds annual Bastille Day parade in Paris – video

stories.title: ICE agent shoots dead Colombian immigrant in Maine
stories.summary: The 26-year-old, who had permission to work in the US, was killed in Biddeford when an officer opened fire, saying his car was a threat. It is the ninth death tied to Trump’s migration crackdown.

stories.title: How India's Lord's win echoes the legacy of its pioneering women cricketers
stories.summary: India won the first women's Test at Lord's, 142 years after its first men's Test.

stories.title: Spectacular drones and fireworks illuminate Eiffel Tower ahead of Bastille Day
stories.summary: Paris staged an early Eiffel Tower fireworks and drone show, reserving Bastille Day to commemorate the 2016 Nice attack and its 86 victims.

stories.title: Zelenskyy joins Macron and European leaders in Paris for Bastille Day
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy joins Emmanuel Macron and European leaders at a Ukraine-focused Bastille Day parade in Paris showcasing military strength.

stories.title: US-Iran conflict widens after deadly tanker attack in Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Commercial shipping has come under attack in the Strait of Hormuz as the US and Iran exchange military action, raising fears the conflict could draw more Gulf states into the fighting.

stories.title: Diamond giant De Beers halts work at flagship South African mine as demand plummets
stories.summary: Production will stop for two years at the mine which employs more than 4,000 people.

stories.title: Tyrannosaurus rex most expensive dinosaur ever sold
stories.summary: The dinosaur skeleton, which roamed the planet 67 million years ago, was sold by Sotheby's in New York.

stories.title: World leaders gather in Doha to honour Qatar's Father Emir Sheikh Hamad
stories.summary: Leaders from Europe, Africa and Asia travelled to Doha to offer condolences to the royal family following the death of Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, who served as Qatar's emir from 1995 until 2013.

stories.title: Trump’s Nato remarks rang hollow – but he recognizes something real | Christopher S Chivvis
stories.summary: US political leaders must be more clear-eyed about our global alliances, without embracing his scorched-earth approach Donald Trump memorably took out a full-page advertisement in multiple newspapers in 1987 charging that America was carrying too much weight for its allies. In his first term he repeated this charge, threatening to withdraw from Nato and berating US allies around the world in the process. Last week’s gathering of Nato’s heads of government in Turkey suggests his approach is runni

stories.title: The Guardian view on Volkswagen’s crisis: another wake-up call for Germany and the EU | Editorial
stories.summary: Robust action is needed to protect European industries from unfair competition. The alternative is social strife amid growing insecurity According to a recent analysis , China enjoys a surplus in its manufactured goods trade with the European Union that is roughly equivalent to Italy’s national income. That trade disparity, it is estimated, continues to grow by about 30% each year. The stark implication, according to a paper from Centre for European Reform, is that Europe, with Germany in the fr

stories.title: France opens terror probe after gun found in Jewish suburb
stories.summary: Police said they found a "military-grade weapon" during the search of a suspicious car parked in a Paris neighborhood with a large Jewish population.

stories.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
stories.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

stories.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
stories.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

stories.title: Peace is possible, Aliyev tells global media forum in Shusha
stories.summary: Azerbaijan's president also told the Shusha Global Media Forum that long-term gas contracts were needed for Baku to expand production, and said that Baku was considering leaving the Council of Europe.

stories.title: Killings continue on Del Monte farm in Kenya, families say, after G4S hired for security
stories.summary: Exclusive: Three men killed in incidents over past year allegedly involving G4S guards, who replaced in-house team after previous deaths Bereaved families and politicians have raised alarm about continued killings on Del Monte’s pineapple farm in Kenya despite the company hiring a British security firm to replace its in-house security team after previous deaths were exposed by the Guardian. The multinational food company appointed G4S to guard the farm, which is estimated to cover at least 40 sq

stories.title: Sinner beats Zverev to retain Wimbledon title
stories.summary: Jannik Sinner retained his Wimbledon title by beating Alexander Zverev in Sunday's men's final. The Italian claims his fifth Grand Slam crown and extends his dominance over the German to 10 straight victories.

stories.title: EU approves new passenger rights for air travel
stories.summary: EU ministers have approved a set of reforms giving more rights to air travelers. Among other things, compensation claims are to be made easier.

stories.title: EU experts urge social media restrictions for kids under 13
stories.summary: An expert panel has recommended limiting access to social media for young children across the EU. The European Commission is expected to present a proposal for legislation on the issue in the coming months.

stories.title: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

stories.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
stories.summary: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

stories.title: New era for Gibraltar with removal of 118-year-old border controls with Spain
stories.summary: It is hoped that free movement between the UK territory and Spain will provide an economic boost.

stories.title: Fire at pub on outskirts of Bangkok kills at least 30 people and injures dozens
stories.summary: The venue has a stage, two bars and a kitchen, with a total capacity of more than 300 people, as well as four fire exits, according to a Facebook post promoting a live show this month.

stories.title: Major wildfire rages south of Paris during heatwave
stories.summary: A major wildfire has spread through France's Fontainebleau forest south of Paris, prompting a large emergency response, disrupting road and rail traffic, and forcing authorities to deploy firefighting aircraft.

stories.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 4,490, officials say, but no number given for missing
stories.summary: Camps for families left homeless have sprung up in stadiums, plazas and on sidewalks in La Guaira as well as nearby Caracas.

stories.title: Azerbaijan considering full withdrawal from Council of Europe, Aliyev says
stories.summary: President Ilham Aliyev said Baku was considering full withdrawal from the 46-member organisation after Azerbaijan's voting rights in PACE were suspended in 2024.

stories.title: Drone footage shows devastation after deadly Spain wildfire
stories.summary: Drone footage shows the devastation left by a deadly wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12 people and burned around 7,000 hectares. Authorities say the blaze has now been brought under control.
